investments.business_description: ATS refers to Aviation Technical Services, Inc., a leading independent provider of aircraft maintenance, repair, and overhaul (MRO) services headquartered in Everett, Washington. The company delivers airframe maintenance, component repair and overhaul, engineering services, fuselage painting, and supply chain support across facilities totaling over 1.2 million square feet. ATS is backed by private equity firm JLL Partners.
investments.end_customers: ATS serves commercial airlines, regional carriers, military operators, aircraft asset managers, and OEMs that need third-party MRO support to keep their fleets airworthy. Customers outsource maintenance to ATS to reduce fixed infrastructure costs, access specialized engineering expertise, and meet regulatory compliance requirements across airframe and component programs.

investments.investment_type: First Lien Senior Secured Loan - Delayed Draw
investments.business_description: Bridger Aerospace Group Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: BAER) is an aerial firefighting services company headquartered in Belgrade, Montana. The company operates the world's largest private Super Scooper fleet — amphibious aircraft capable of scooping up to 1,412 gallons of water from lakes and rivers for wildfire suppression — along with air attack and surveillance aircraft. Bridger has supported wildfire operations across 24 U.S. states and two Canadian provinces.
investments.end_customers: Bridger's primary customers are U.S. federal agencies including the U.S. Forest Service and Department of the Interior, as well as state governments, that contract aerial firefighting capacity to suppress wildfires. Government agencies contract Bridger's aircraft on a seasonal and multi-year basis because owning and operating a scooper fleet in-house is prohibitively capital-intensive.

investments.business_description: Bridger Aerospace Group Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: BAER) is an aerial firefighting services company headquartered in Belgrade, Montana. The company operates the world's largest private Super Scooper fleet — amphibious aircraft capable of scooping up to 1,412 gallons of water from lakes and rivers for wildfire suppression — along with air attack and surveillance aircraft. Government agencies contract Bridger's aircraft on a seasonal and multi-year basis because owning and operating a scooper fleet in-house is prohibitively capital-intensive.

investments.business_description: BTX Precision is a private equity-backed (L Squared Capital Partners) platform of precision manufacturing companies delivering build-to-print components for aerospace, defense, space, medical, and semiconductor customers. The platform combines more than 10 acquired manufacturing units across over 700,000 square feet of production space, offering capabilities including CNC milling and turning, Swiss machining, laser micro-machining, 3D metal printing, aluminum vacuum brazing, and EDM. All operating entities are ITAR-registered and AS9100D-certified.
investments.end_customers: BTX serves prime defense contractors, aerospace OEMs, and their Tier 1 suppliers that need mission-critical tight-tolerance components machined from exotic alloys — including nickel superalloys, titanium, and high-strength steels — for applications such as jet engines, naval systems, and ground defense platforms. Customers rely on BTX for complex geometries that require specialized processes they cannot cost-effectively perform in-house.

investments.business_description: Forward Slope, Inc. is a San Diego, California-based defense technology company (founded 2002, backed by Trive Capital) that develops mission-critical software, surveillance systems, and command-and-control (C2) solutions for the U.S. Navy and other national security customers. The company specializes in tactical visualization, geospatial mapping, data science, cybersecurity, and turnkey global surveillance platforms. Through its acquisition of Soar Technology (SoarTech), Forward Slope also provides AI-based decision support and autonomous systems software.
investments.end_customers: Forward Slope's customers are U.S. Department of Defense agencies — primarily the Navy — as well as intelligence and national security agencies that require real-time situational awareness, C2 integration, and surveillance software for maritime and joint operations. Government customers choose Forward Slope for its deep domain expertise in integrating disparate sensor feeds into unified operational pictures for warfighters.

investments.investment_type: First Lien Senior Secured Loan - Revolver
investments.business_description: GSP Holdings, LLC is the holding company for G.S. Precision, Inc., a Brattleboro, Vermont-based manufacturer of complex, high-precision machined components and specialty hardware used primarily in aerospace engines and defense systems. Founded in 1958, the company is backed by AE Industrial Partners and has grown through acquisitions including SMC Aerospace and F.T. Gearing, expanding its capabilities into highly engineered tubes, machined components, and specialized aerospace gears.
investments.end_customers: G.S. Precision's customers are jet engine OEMs and defense prime contractors — including companies such as Pratt & Whitney, GE Aerospace, and Rolls-Royce — that source complex, tight-tolerance components for new production programs and aftermarket repair. Engine manufacturers rely on GSP because its multi-decade precision machining expertise, proprietary processes, and certified quality systems make it a qualified supplier for mission-critical rotating and structural hardware.

investments.business_description: Heads Up Technologies, Inc. (HUT) is a Carrollton, Texas-based provider of cockpit, cabin, and aviation electrical solutions for the business, commercial, and defense aviation markets, backed by JLL Partners. The company's product portfolio spans cockpit enhancements, cabin management systems, LED lighting, in-flight entertainment, and flight deck safety systems. Through acquisitions of STG Aerospace and ALTO Aviation, HUT has expanded into aircraft cabin lighting and interior solutions.
investments.end_customers: HUT's customers are aircraft OEMs, completion centers, and operators in the business aviation, commercial airline, and defense segments that require proprietary cockpit and cabin system upgrades, new production installations, or aftermarket retrofits. Customers value HUT's breadth of integrated solutions — from lighting to control interfaces — that reduce the number of vendors needed to configure a fully equipped aircraft interior.

investments.investment_type: First Lien Senior Secured Loan - Revolver
investments.business_description: Mach Acquisition R/C is the revolving credit facility tranche of debt held by BCSF in Mach Industries, Inc., a Huntington Beach, California-based defense technology company (founded 2023) developing expendable munitions and autonomous unmanned aerial vehicles for the U.S. military. Mach's primary products include the 'Glide' precision munition and the 'Viper' jet-powered VTOL UAV, produced at its own vertically integrated factory. The company is backed by venture investors including Khosla Ventures, Bedrock Capital, and Sequoia Capital.
investments.end_customers: Mach Industries sells to U.S. military branches — including the Army — and defense procurement agencies seeking low-cost, rapidly scalable expendable munitions and autonomous platforms that can be acquired outside traditional slow-moving defense acquisition programs via OTA contracts. The military's demand is driven by the need to replenish munition stockpiles and field next-generation autonomous strike systems at a fraction of the cost of legacy systems.

investments.business_description: Mach Acquisition T/L is the first-lien term loan tranche of debt held by BCSF in Mach Industries, Inc., a Huntington Beach, California-based defense technology company (founded 2023) developing expendable munitions and autonomous unmanned aerial vehicles for the U.S. military. Mach's primary products include the 'Glide' precision munition and the 'Viper' jet-powered VTOL UAV, produced at its own vertically integrated factory. The company is backed by venture investors including Khosla Ventures, Bedrock Capital, and Sequoia Capital.
investments.end_customers: Mach Industries sells to U.S. military branches — including the Army — and defense procurement agencies seeking low-cost, rapidly scalable expendable munitions and autonomous platforms that can be acquired outside traditional slow-moving defense acquisition programs via OTA contracts. The military's demand is driven by the need to replenish munition stockpiles and field next-generation autonomous strike systems at a fraction of the cost of legacy systems.

investments.business_description: Precision Ultimate Holdings is a holding company for a platform of precision aerospace and defense component manufacturers. The underlying businesses perform tight-tolerance CNC machining, electrical discharge machining (EDM), and fabrication of complex metal parts for space, defense, and aerospace programs, all certified to AS9100 and ITAR standards. The platform was built through a series of acquisitions in the Texas and Kansas markets beginning in 2022 by sponsors CIC Partners and Juniper Capital Management.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are prime aerospace and defense contractors and government programs that require flight-critical and defense-critical machined components. End demand flows from U.S. Department of Defense programs, space launch vehicle manufacturers, and commercial aerospace OEMs who require certified suppliers for precision-machined structural and mechanical parts.

investments.business_description: Precision Ultimate Holdings is a holding company for a platform of precision aerospace and defense component manufacturers. The underlying businesses perform tight-tolerance CNC machining, electrical discharge machining (EDM), and fabrication of complex metal parts for space, defense, and aerospace programs, all certified to AS9100 and ITAR standards. The platform was built through a series of acquisitions in the Texas and Kansas markets beginning in 2022 by sponsors CIC Partners and Juniper Capital Management.
investments.end_customers: Primary customers are prime aerospace and defense contractors and government programs that require flight-critical and defense-critical machined components. End demand flows from U.S. Department of Defense programs, space launch vehicle manufacturers, and commercial aerospace OEMs who require certified suppliers for precision-machined structural and mechanical parts.

investments.business_description: Robinson Helicopter Company, headquartered in Torrance, California, is the largest manufacturer of civil helicopters in North America. The company designs, manufactures, assembles, and flight-tests its entire product line at its Torrance factory, including the two-seat piston-powered R22, the four-seat R44, and the five-seat turbine-powered R66. As of 2025, Robinson has produced more than 14,000 helicopters, with roughly two-thirds of new production exported internationally.
investments.end_customers: Robinson sells primarily to flight training schools, private owners, law enforcement agencies, agricultural operators, and tour companies around the world. The R22 and R44 are the dominant platforms for ab initio helicopter flight training globally, while the R66 serves commercial operators and high-net-worth individual buyers seeking a turbine helicopter at a competitive price point.

investments.business_description: Saturn Purchaser Corp. is a KKR-backed acquisition vehicle in the commercial and professional services sector whose specific underlying operating company could not be independently confirmed from publicly available sources. The KKR commercial services portfolio includes businesses in home services (Neighborly), healthcare services (BrightSpring), and other outsourced commercial services. The 'Saturn' naming is an opaque holding company designation used at transaction close.
investments.end_customers: Without confirmation of the underlying operating company, the specific end customer base cannot be determined with confidence. Given the commercial and professional services classification and KKR's portfolio focus, the business likely serves enterprise or government clients with recurring outsourced service contracts.

investments.business_description: Whitcraft-Paradigm refers to the combined entity of Whitcraft Group and Paradigm Precision, which merged in early 2023 under the name Pursuit Aerospace, backed by Clayton, Dubilier & Rice and Greenbriar Equity Group. The combined company is a scaled manufacturer of complex, high-tolerance engine components — including precision machined parts, fabrications, investment castings, and complex assemblies — primarily for commercial and military gas turbine engines. With more than 2,500 employees and over $800 million in annual revenues, it is one of the largest independent aviation engine component suppliers.
investments.end_customers: Customers are the major commercial aero engine OEMs — primarily GE Aerospace, Pratt & Whitney, Rolls-Royce, and Safran — who source outsourced precision components for both new production engines and aftermarket MRO. Demand is driven by commercial air travel recovery, new narrowbody/widebody programs, and military engine upgrades.

investments.business_description: American Trailer Rental Group (ATRG), rebranded as Warehouse on Wheels in February 2024, is the leading provider of commercial mobile storage solutions in North America. Backed by Wind Point Partners, the company operates 36 locations with a fleet of more than 35,000 trailers and 5,000 ground-level storage containers, serving manufacturing, distribution, retail, and third-party logistics customers. ATRG grew through a buy-and-build strategy, combining 10 regional trailer rental brands.
investments.end_customers: End customers are manufacturers, distributors, retailers, and 3PLs that need supplemental trailer storage capacity for overflow inventory, peak season demand, construction material storage, or disaster relief operations. Customers pay recurring rental fees, creating a subscription-like revenue model tied to industrial activity and supply chain activity levels.

investments.business_description: Plaskolite, owned by Pritzker Private Capital in partnership with the founding Dunn family, is North America's largest manufacturer of transparent thermoplastic sheet products including acrylic (PMMA), polycarbonate, ABS, PVC, HDPE, and PETG sheet, extruded profiles, and PMMA polymers. Founded in 1950 in Columbus, Ohio, Plaskolite produces customized sheet products used in windows, doors, lighting fixtures, signage, point-of-purchase displays, transportation glazing, security glazing, and bath products. The company has expanded through acquisitions including the assets of Plazit-Polygal and a cell-cast acrylic manufacturing facility from Trinseo.
investments.end_customers: Plaskolite's customers are fabricators, distributors, glaziers, sign manufacturers, and OEMs across construction, retail, transportation, and industrial end markets who convert thermoplastic sheet into finished glazing, display, and structural components. The company supplies both the wholesale distribution channel and direct OEM accounts across North America and internationally.

investments.company: AGS American Glass Services Acquisition, LLC

investments.business_description: Senneca Holdings (acquired by Kohlberg & Company from Audax Private Equity in 2018, originally Chase Industries) is a manufacturer of specialty industrial doors, headquartered in Cincinnati, Ohio, operating twelve manufacturing facilities. The company's portfolio of brands — Chase, Eliason, TMI, Thermoseal, SubZero, Curtron, and others — covers traffic doors, flexible strip doors, specialty glass doors, cold storage doors, and data center enclosures. All products are built-to-order for specific industrial and commercial applications.
investments.end_customers: Senneca's customers are facility managers, construction contractors, and procurement teams at food processing plants, cold storage warehouses, distribution centers, retail and food service facilities, pharmaceutical manufacturing plants, and data centers — all of which require specialty doors to manage temperature, hygiene, traffic flow, and energy efficiency. The product line's critical function in food safety compliance and cold chain integrity makes it a recurring replacement and new construction purchase.

investments.business_description: G702 Buyer, Inc. is the operating vehicle for a Genstar Capital-backed platform in the systems and information management sector, with PitchBook classifying it under Systems and Information Management. The company has raised approximately $12 million and operates in commercial and professional services. Based on the Genstar Capital profile and the AIA G702 naming convention (an industry-standard construction payment application form), the underlying business likely provides software or document management services for the architecture, engineering, and construction industry.
investments.end_customers: End customers are likely architects, engineers, construction managers, and contractors who need standardized billing and contract documentation tools. These professionals rely on platforms like this to generate code-compliant payment applications and project documentation at scale.

investments.business_description: WU Holdco, Inc. is the holding company for Weiman Products, a Gurnee, Illinois-based manufacturer and distributor of specialty surface cleaning and care products. The Weiman brand portfolio includes Weiman (stainless steel, granite, wood), Goo Gone (adhesive remover), Magic (multi-surface cleaners), Stone Care International, Wright's (silver polish), and Gonzo Natural Magic for consumers, plus Urnex and Five Star for commercial coffee equipment cleaning. The business was sold by Cortec Group to Carlyle Group and TA Associates; the ARCC holding likely reflects a debt investment from a prior capital structure.
investments.end_customers: Consumers purchasing specialty cleaning products for high-value home surfaces through major retailers like Home Depot, Lowe's, Walmart, Target, and Amazon. investments.business_description: ASP-r-pac is the acquisition holding structure for r-pac International, a leading global provider of branding and packaging solutions to the retail apparel supply chain, with backing from Ares Management (via American Securities as co-investor). r-pac International, founded in 1987 and headquartered in New York City, supplies RFID-enabled tags, labels, hangtags, belly bands, and supply chain solutions to retailers and brand owners, operating across 30 worldwide locations. The company is a leader in item-level RFID tagging for apparel from source-to-store.
investments.end_customers: r-pac's customers are apparel brands, fashion retailers, and their sourcing/manufacturing partners who need branded packaging, labeling, and RFID-enabled inventory tracking solutions across global supply chains. Retailers and brands use r-pac's tags and labels to achieve near 100% in-store inventory accuracy and full SKU-level supply chain visibility.

investments.business_description: Precision Concepts International (PCI), originally backed by ONCAP (Onex Corporation's lower mid-market PE platform) since 2017 and subsequently sold to The Sterling Group in July 2025, is a designer and manufacturer of specialty rigid consumer packaging solutions including sticks, jars, vials, closures, spouts, bottles, and tubes, serving personal care, household, food and beverage, and nutraceutical end markets. The company is headquartered in Huntersville, North Carolina, and has grown through six strategic acquisitions under ONCAP ownership. ONCAP retained a minority position post-sale.
investments.end_customers: Customers are branded consumer goods companies in personal care (cosmetics, skincare), household products, food and beverage, and dietary supplements that source specialty rigid packaging components from PCI for their branded products. Packaging specifications are often custom-designed per client brand, making PCI an integrated development and manufacturing partner rather than a commodity supplier.

investments.business_description: Revalize is a cloud-based software platform for manufacturers and their distribution channels, providing an integrated suite of Configure, Price, Quote (CPQ), product selection, CAD/PLM visualization, and Product Information Management (PIM) tools built specifically for industrial and specialty manufacturers, created by TA Associates and HG Capital in 2021 through the combination of multiple manufacturing software companies. The platform helps 15,000+ customers globally design, configure, quote, and sell over $8 billion in products monthly, spanning markets such as HVAC, pumps and flow control, electrical, foodservice equipment, and specialty industrial goods. Revalize has grown aggressively through acquisitions of vertical-specific software companies serving niche manufacturing end markets.
investments.end_customers: Revalize's customers are manufacturers and their sales channels — independent reps, distributors, and direct sales teams — that sell configurable, engineered-to-order products where accurate quoting requires complex technical configuration rules. Sales engineers, inside sales teams, and channel partners use Revalize's CPQ tools to generate technically valid and priced proposals for custom equipment without requiring direct engineering involvement for every quote. The company's deep vertical focus in industrial equipment means its software is a mission-critical revenue workflow tool for mid-market manufacturers.

investments.business_description: Thrasio is one of the largest Amazon FBA (Fulfilled by Amazon) brand aggregators, founded in 2018, which acquires third-party private-label brands selling on Amazon and other e-commerce channels and then optimizes them through supply chain improvements, marketing, and product development. The company at its peak had acquired over 200 brands across categories such as home goods, health, outdoor, and pets. Thrasio filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy in February 2024 and emerged from restructuring in June 2024 under new CEO Stephanie Fox.
investments.end_customers: End consumers who shop on Amazon and direct-to-consumer websites are the ultimate buyers of Thrasio's portfolio of everyday consumer goods. Thrasio's own direct customers are the original brand founders who sell their FBA businesses to Thrasio in exchange for cash consideration.

investments.business_description: Datix Bidco Limited is the holding structure for RLDatix, the world's largest software company exclusively dedicated to patient safety, healthcare quality, and governance, risk, and compliance (GRC). RLDatix was formed in 2018 through the merger of RL Solutions and Datix, and was subsequently backed by Nordic Capital (which invested in January 2022), Five Arrows, and TA Associates. The company's platform covers incident reporting, risk management, infection surveillance, peer review, claims management, and workforce scheduling for healthcare organizations across 19 countries. Hg Capital was a previous investor in Allocate Software, which RLDatix acquired.
investments.end_customers: Customers are hospitals, health systems, NHS trusts, and healthcare networks globally that use RLDatix to manage patient safety incidents, comply with accreditation requirements, track infection rates, and manage clinical governance. The regulatory mandate around incident reporting makes the software essential infrastructure for accredited healthcare organizations.

investments.business_description: Spotless Brands, backed by Access Holdings since 2020, is a super-regional express car wash platform operating 220+ locations across 13 states (including Arizona, Colorado, Maryland, Virginia, D.C., Ohio, Tennessee, and others) under brands including Flagship Carwash, Cobblestone Auto Spa, Cobblestone Car Wash, Ultimate Shine Car Wash, and Okie Express Auto Wash. The company operates primarily express tunnel car washes with monthly membership models and has grown rapidly through both acquisitions and greenfield development. A recent acquisition of Pete's Express Car Wash (six Philadelphia-area sites) extended the company into the Pennsylvania market.
investments.end_customers: End customers are vehicle owners seeking affordable, fast, express car wash services, particularly unlimited monthly membership plans that incentivize frequent visits. Property of the membership model means Spotless generates recurring monthly subscription revenue from a large installed base of member vehicles, smoothing out weather-driven variability in single-wash demand.

investments.business_description: VASA Fitness, backed by Silver Oak Services Partners (initial recapitalization in November 2014), is a high-value, low-price (HVLP) fitness club chain operating 70+ locations across Utah, Colorado, Arizona, Oklahoma, Nebraska, Indiana, Illinois, and Wisconsin, making it the largest gym operator in the Utah and Colorado markets. The company offers large-format clubs (full-service locations with pools, group fitness, personal training, basketball, and childcare) and smaller express clubs at affordable monthly membership prices starting at $14.99/month. VASA acquired 17 Gold's Gym locations in 2014 as part of its expansion strategy.
investments.end_customers: Members are value-conscious fitness consumers, primarily in the Mountain West and Midwest, who want access to premium amenities (pools, childcare, group fitness studios, basketball courts) at price points well below boutique fitness studios. The affordable monthly membership model attracts a broad demographic spanning students, families, and working adults seeking accessible fitness in their local communities.
